verdict.evidence: The source says 'The Ministry of Defence is sending a proposal... for a consultation round' (present continuous, ongoing/imminent), while the output says 'has submitted a proposal... for consultation' (present perfect, completed action). This is a minor tense drift from an in-progress action to a completed one. All other temporal elements (the date 2 April 2026, future tense for proposed changes, present tense for current law) are accurately preserved.
